name: Marbled Double Chocolate Cheesecake Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line an 8-inch or 9-inch square baking pan with foil, extending edges over the sides of the pan.
3. In a small mixing bowl, beat butter and 1/2 cup sugar until smooth. Add salt.
4. Stir together flour and cocoa; gradually add to butter mixture, beating until soft dough is formed.
5. Press dough onto bottom of prepared pan.
6. In another bowl, beat cream cheese and remaining 1/2 cup sugar until smooth.
7. Add eggs and vanilla; blend well.
8. Mix 1 cup of the batter with the chocolate topping in a separate bowl, stirring until well blended.
9. Pour this topped batter over the dough.
10. Stir melted chocolate into the remaining batter.
11. Drop tablespoonfuls of the reserved batter onto the top to create swirls.
12. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until the cheesecake is firm and slightly puffed.
13. Remove from oven and cool completely on a wire rack.
14. Refrigerate until serving.
15. To serve, lift the cheesecake from the pan using the foil edges and cut into squares. Garnish as desired.
